newsSinceJun6: No material company news since June 6. Escode disposal to TDR Capital (gross £309.1m, ~£253m net, £185m planned shareholder return) was announced completed on May 29, pre-window. Since June 6 only routine Takeover Code Form 8.3/8.5 dealing disclosures (e.g., Vanguard at 5.05%) confirming the stock remains in an offer period while the board reviews all options for the retained Cyber business, including a potential sale of the whole company. Hard catalyst imminent: unaudited H1 (six months to March 31, 2026) results due June 11. Today's -1.66% is drift, not fundamental.

sources.label: Investing.com - NCC Group completes Escode sale, plans £185m shareholder return (May 29, 2026)
Form 8.3 - The Vanguard Group: NCC Group plc (offer-period dealing disclosure)
Investing.com - NCC Group confirms strategic review of cyber business

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down move already reconciled the adjusted-score rank; nothing genuinely new since June 6 to justify another move. A -1.66% drift into the June 11 H1 results, with a pending £185m capital return (~45% of market cap) and an open offer-period/strategic review that could end in a whole-company sale, marginally improves the asymmetry on this under-owned, un-rerated (+5.8% YTD) small cap, so a small positive delta without a rank change.
verdicts.bull: Pure-play cyber assurance post-Escode with ~£253m net cash landed, £185m committed to shareholders, and a live strategic review that explicitly includes a potential sale of the entire company - takeout optionality on a $0.56B name nobody owns.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but the AI-supply-chain linkage (security testing/assurance for AI infrastructure) is indirect; everything now hinges on the June 11 H1 print and the form/timing of the capital return.
verdicts.bear: Weak H1 organic numbers from the retained Cyber business on June 11 could reprice the stub down, and the strategic review could conclude with no sale and a slow buyback rather than a clean takeout, leaving a subscale UK services business.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June 11, 2026 H1 results plus any update on the Cyber business review and the mechanism of the £185m shareholder return.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Disappointing retained-Cyber trading or a no-sale conclusion to the strategic review removes the takeout premium embedded in the offer-period setup.
